'''Julien Rinaldi''' (born 27 April 1979) is a French former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Catalans Dragons]], [[London Broncos]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]]. He also played for and coaches the [[Villeneuve Leopards]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] in France. Rinaldi was a [[France national rugby league team|France]] international. His preferred position is {{rlp|sh}}, however, he has spent most of his career at {{rlp|hk}}.

Rinaldi has also played for the [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Sunshine Coast Sea Eagles]], the [[Catalans Dragons]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref>[http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=13749 engage Super League] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120322165018/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=13749 |date=2012-03-22 }}. Superleague.co.uk. Retrieved on 2011-05-25.</ref>

==Background==
Rinaldi was born in [[Villeneuve-lès-Avignon]], [[France]].

==Playing career==
He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#France|France squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="France name official squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |title=France name official squad |publisher=RLWC2008 |date=2008-09-24 |accessdate=2008-10-03 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080930072635/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |archivedate=2008-09-30 }}</ref>
Rinaldi signed a one-month deal to play for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] after returning from France.<ref>[https://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_6211551,00.html Rinaldi joins Wildcats|Sky Sports|Rugby League|News]. Sky Sports (2010-06-17). Retrieved on 2011-05-25.</ref> He then signed a new contract to remain with Wakefield Trinity until the end of the 2011 season.
